description_long: This object is a flintlock pistol, featuring a curved wooden grip and a metal barrel with ornate engravings. The lock mechanism is prominently visible, including the hammer, frizzen, and pan, all crafted from dark metal. The trigger guard and trigger are also made of metal, with the guard curving elegantly beneath the grip. The barrel is relatively short and cylindrical, with a smoothbore muzzle and no visible rifling. The overall design is reminiscent of 18th to early 19th-century firearms, with a focus on both functionality and decorative elements. The wood appears polished and dark, contrasting with the matte and slightly glossy metal components.
description: A flintlock pistol with a curved wooden grip, ornate metal lockwork, and a short, engraved barrel. The design is both functional and decorative, typical of historical firearms.
description_view_0: The pistol is seen from the muzzle end, showing the circular barrel opening and the rounded end of the grip.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the curved wooden grip, ornate lock mechanism, and engraved barrel.
description_view_2: The pistol is viewed from the rear, displaying the hammer, frizzen, and the back of the grip.
description_view_3: The right side view reveals the trigger, trigger guard, and additional details of the lock mechanism and barrel engravings.
